meaning: n.演说家所做之事adj.有关演说家的
examples: James has played in several "barnstorming" games over the past month of the NBA lockout, but this is the first time he's been a part of hosting one.
The whispers began a few weeks ago, with reports coming from the team monitoring the Cassini spacecraft, which has been orbiting Saturn and barnstorming its moons since 2004.
McCain and Feingold have been barnstorming the states of Senators whose votes are in doubt.
One tempting explanation for United's barnstorming finishes is the phenomenon commonly known as Fergie Time.
Coast-to-coast barnstorming, with all its speech-making and flesh-pressing, amounts to a sort of decathlon.
